_comment: VCF interoperability and HCL data. Sources: Broadcom release notes, vSphere interop matrix, VMware Compatibility Guide.

9.1.description: VCF 9.1 requires all core components on the 9.1.x release stream. Mixed major versions are not supported.

9.0.description: VCF 9.0 requires all components on the 9.0.x release stream. Mixed major versions are not supported.

5.2.description: VCF 5.2 requires vCenter/ESXi 8.0.2+, NSX 4.1+, SDDC Manager 5.2.x.

5.1.description: VCF 5.1 requires vCenter/ESXi 8.0.1+, NSX 4.0+, SDDC Manager 5.1.x.

upgrade_order: VCF Operations (via PAK file — must be first)
prerequisites: Temporary IP address required per vCenter appliance
vCenter must not be joined to an AD domain
TPM 2.0 firmware verified or disabled in BIOS
ESX hardware on HCL with latest BIOS/firmware
Backups of SDDC Manager + all vCenter/NSX Manager appliances
No failed workflows in SDDC Manager

workflow_rules.note: All upgrades with SDDC Manager must use the VCF Installer lifecycle workflows. Reference: KB440630.

workflow_rules.workflow: VCF Installer workflows ONLY — independent component upgrades are NOT supported
workflow_rules.note: When NSX 4.x is present, only VCF Installer workflows with convert/import operations are supported. Attempting independent component upgrades will fail. Reference: KB440630.
Aria Automation 8.x (with or without NSX 4.x) requires VCF Installer workflows only. Independent upgrade is not supported. Reference: KB440630.

workflow_rules.workflow: VCF Installer workflows — VMware Identity Manager requires manual post-upgrade migration
workflow_rules.note: vRealize Suite Lifecycle Manager 8.x: use VCF Installer workflows. Post-upgrade action required: manually migrate VMware Identity Manager to the new Identity Broker service in VCF Management Services. Reference: KB440630.

workflow_rules.workflow: Enable Log Management Service; migrate logs to VCF Log Management 9.1
workflow_rules.note: Aria Log Insight 8.x: enable the Log Management Service in VCF 9.1 and migrate existing log data. Reference: KB440630.

workflow_rules.workflow: Aria Network Insight must be upgraded via VCF Operations Fleet LCM workflows ONLY
workflow_rules.note: Aria Network Insight 8.x upgrade is only supported through VCF Operations Fleet LCM workflows, not independently. Reference: KB440630.

workflow_rules.workflow: Use VCF Installer workflows for VCF/VVF, OR download VCF Operations with separate license server
workflow_rules.note: Environments with only vCenter/ESX 8.x or 9.0.x (no SDDC Manager) can use VCF Installer workflows or independently download VCF Operations with a separate license server install. Reference: KB440630.

fleet_management_appliance.note: Fleet Management Appliance (VCF 9.0) CANNOT be used for 9.1 upgrades and must be decommissioned after upgrading the environment. It is replaced by Fleet Lifecycle and SDDC Lifecycle services within VCF Management Services.

vmware_identity_broker.note: VMware Identity Broker (9.0.x external cluster) is consolidated into VCF Management Services in 9.1. Deployments on the management network: no downtime. Deployments on a non-management network: requires Broadcom Support guidance and involves downtime.

vcf_license_server.note: A centralized VCF License Server is a new required component in VCF 9.1. Must be deployed as part of the upgrade process.

vcf_management_services_requirements.note: VCF Management Services requires a minimum of 12 IP addresses on the management network initially. Maximum 30 total. Internal IP range defaults to 198.18.0.0/15 but can be changed to 240.0.0.0/15 or 250.0.0.0/15 if conflicting with existing infrastructure.

9.1.notes: Direct upgrade to VCF 9.1 supported from VCF 9.0 or VCF 5.2. Versions earlier than 5.2 must stage through 5.2 first. VCF Operations (Aria Operations) must be upgraded before all other components. Workflow type is determined by which components are present — see workflow_rules. Source: KB440630 (Broadcom).

9.0.notes: Direct upgrade to VCF 9.0 requires source VCF 5.2.x. Earlier versions require staged intermediate upgrades.

5.2.notes: Direct upgrade to VCF 5.2 from 5.0 or 5.1 is supported.

hardware_hcl._comment: true=certified, false=not certified, 'warning'=verify on Broadcom HCL. Source: VMware Compatibility Guide / Broadcom Compatibility Matrix.

boot_devices: USB boot devices (removal planned in next major release)
SD card boot devices (removal planned in next major release)
ESX hosts without a persistent storage device for OSDATA partition
ESX hosts with storage device < 32 GB
network_adapters: Marvell/Aquantia (Atlantic) 10GbE USB/PCIe NICs — no driver support
AMD Solarflare X2 series Ethernet adapters
Cisco VIC 1200 series — no Enhanced Data Path support
Cisco VIC 1300 series — no Enhanced Data Path support
features: vSphere Cluster Services (vCLS) UI fully removed in 9.1

hardware_support: Intel NIC E825 for Xeon Gen 6 (Granite Rapids-D)
Intel TDX GA support for Xeon Gen 5 (Emerald Rapids) and later
AMD EPYC IOMMU virtualization with DirectPath I/O for Zen 3 (Milan) and later
upgrade_notes: vCenter and NSX Manager are upgraded in a single combined step in 9.1
VCF Operations must be upgraded via PAK file before all other components
vSphere 8 + Aria Operations 8 have a separate migration path to VCF 9.1
